title: Discovery Blasts Off to Space
story: Whoosh! The space shuttle Discovery roared to life, launching into orbit on November 22, 1989. This exciting mission, called STS-33, marked the 63rd time humans had ventured into space. As Discovery soared high above Earth, its crew prepared for a secret mission. The shuttle's powerful engines pushed it faster and faster until it reached the inky blackness of space.
activity_question: What was the name of the space shuttle that launched on this mission?
follow_up_question: Why do you think some space missions are kept secret?

title: Space Shuttle Columbia's Epic Journey Home
story: Soaring through the skies, the mighty Space Shuttle Columbia made its triumphant return in 1982. After an incredible mission, this amazing spacecraft touched down at Kelly Air Force Base in Texas before completing its journey to Kennedy Space Center on November 22. It was a day of celebration for NASA and space fans everywhere!
activity_question: Where did the Space Shuttle Columbia land before reaching Kennedy Space Center?
follow_up_question: Why do you think space shuttles sometimes land at different locations before reaching their final destination?

title: Space Shuttle Columbia's Big Move
story: In 1985, the Space Shuttle Columbia embarked on an exciting journey. On November 22, it rolled into the massive Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center in Florida. This wasn't a launch, but a crucial step called 'mating.' Columbia was being joined with its fuel tank and boosters, preparing for its upcoming mission, STS 61-C.
activity_question: Where did the Space Shuttle Columbia move to on November 22, 1985?
follow_up_question: Why do you think they call joining the shuttle with its fuel tank and boosters 'mating'?

title: Vasco da Gama's Daring Ocean Adventure
story: Brave explorer Vasco da Gama set sail on an epic journey. On November 22, 1497, his ships rounded the Cape of Good Hope in South Africa. This was a huge moment! It meant da Gama had found a sea route from Europe to India. His voyage opened up new trade paths and changed the world. Sailing into unknown waters took great courage and skill.
activity_question: What did Vasco da Gama's ships round on their journey?
follow_up_question: Why was finding a sea route to India important for European traders?

title: Digging Deep: The Simplon Tunnel Begins
story: Pickaxes struck rock as workers began carving the Simplon tunnel in 1898. This massive project would burrow through the mighty Alps, connecting Italy and Switzerland by rail. On November 22, engineers launched their ambitious plan to create the world's longest railroad tunnel. It was a monumental task, but one that would revolutionize travel across Europe.
activity_question: What mountain range did the Simplon tunnel go through?
follow_up_question: Why do you think building tunnels through mountains is important for transportation?

title: A Massive Welcome to New Americans
story: Cheers erupted as nearly forty thousand people became US citizens in 1985. On November 22, the largest swearing-in ceremony ever took place. Immigrants from many countries raised their hands, pledged allegiance, and officially joined the American family. It was a powerful moment of unity and hope for these new Americans.
activity_question: What happened during this record-breaking ceremony?
activity_choices: A giant apple pie was baked
follow_up_question: Why do you think so many people wanted to become US citizens?

title: Bridging Nations: US-China Travel Ban Ends
story: Ping-pong diplomacy paved the way for a historic change in 1972. After 22 years of closed doors, the United States lifted its travel ban to China on November 22. This move allowed Americans to visit the world's most populous country for the first time in over two decades, opening up new opportunities for cultural exchange and understanding between these two powerful nations.
activity_question: What sport helped lead to the end of the US travel ban to China?
follow_up_question: Why do you think sports can help improve relationships between countries?

title: Paws for a Cause: Humane Society Begins
story: Animal lovers, rejoice! In 1954, a group of caring people banded together to protect our furry friends. On November 22, the Humane Society of the United States was born in Washington, D.C. This organization fights for the rights of animals, from pets to wildlife. They work to stop cruelty and make sure all creatures are treated with kindness.
activity_question: Where was the Humane Society of the United States formed?
follow_up_question: Can you think of ways to help animals in your community?

title: Cricket Legends Make Their Grand Entrance
story: Two cricket superstars burst onto the scene in Bangalore. Gordon Greenidge and Viv Richards made their Test Cricket debuts on November 22, 1974. These talented players would go on to become legends of the game, thrilling fans with their incredible skills and powerful batting. Their first match marked the beginning of two remarkable careers that would change the face of cricket forever.
activity_question: Where did Gordon Greenidge and Viv Richards make their Test Cricket debuts?
bonus_fact: Test Cricket is the longest form of the sport, with matches lasting up to five days.
